pct_autism.note: Derived: special-education / autism enrollment (CDE SPED-PS, ages 3-22) as a share of TK-12 census enrollment (CDE Census Day Enrollment). Both are district aggregates that include authorized charter schools.

pct_iep.note: Derived: special-education / autism enrollment (CDE SPED-PS, ages 3-22) as a share of TK-12 census enrollment (CDE Census Day Enrollment). Both are district aggregates that include authorized charter schools.

oah_cases_5yr_total.note: OAH special-education decisions naming this district, 2022-2026. All-time published total: 169. Autism count is a keyword heuristic (decision text mentions autism/ASD).

district_web_research.additional_findings: Largest school district in California and second-largest in the United States. Division of Special Education publishes a multi-tier autism program structure: an Autism Core Program (Special Day Program model that pairs students with autism with peers for communication-skills development) and a High Functioning Autism Core Program (supports students with HFA in general-ed classes while building communication skills). District-wide Career and Transition Centers provide Adult Transition Programs (employment preparation, independent living skills, social skills) to students ages 18-22 on the alternate curriculum. District also has a Positive Behavior Training arm. BCBA credentials are not explicitly named on the public Division of Special Education pages.

autism_specific_classrooms.name: Autism Core Program (Special Day Program with peer pairing for communication skills)
High Functioning Autism Core Program (general-ed with communication-skills support)

pupil_services_per_100_iep.note: Derived: certificated staff count (CDE Staff Race/Ethnicity, Census Day) normalized to enrollment (Census Day) or IEP count (SPED-PS, ages 3-22). PSV (pupil services) bundles counselors, psychologists, SLPs, social workers, and nurses — CDE no longer publishes role-granular district-level staff counts.
